posts.name: Dennis Mario Roy
posts.text:
*** Discussion title: Higgs Combination
Dear experts,
I'd like ask two questions about some observations I made while using combine:
First, I used my workspace to make a 1D scan over the POI. I used this command (with combine tag v8.2.0):
combine -d WSRun2_unblind.indep.root --algo grid -P sigma --setParameterRanges sigma=0,0.05 --alignEdges 1 --points 51 --setParameters fvbf=1.0 --freezeParameters fvbf --cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 0 --cminFallbackAlgo Minuit2,0:1 -M MultiDimFit -m 650
I put the workspace in [1]. The result I get from the scan is [2]. You can see that there's a bump at the minimum. The peak of this bump is the best fit from the initial fit (with NLL=0) before the POI is scanned over the given POI range. The NLL values from the 1D scan surrounding the best fit value are however negative, implying that they were fitted better. Another thing to point out, is that the uncertainty on the POI from the initial fit (or from MultiDimFit with "--algo none", which I think is equivalent) is lower than what can be read from the plot (in this example the uncertainty on the POI is 0.001752, while in the plot it's closer to 0.003).
It seems to me that the accuracy of the fit in the initial fit to data wasn't optimal. Could this be because I'm using the option "--cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 0"? I use this option because there are very many nuisances in my workspace, so it's much faster, and in the past I also found the fits to be more robust and failing less. Though I find it strange that, even when using this same option to scan the range of the POI, NLL values surrounding the best fit are still smaller.
Secondly, I also made a strange observation with the option "--cminFallbackAlgo Minuit2,0:1". In my workspace, the fits sometimes fail with the status 3 or 4, because the EDM value is still too large after the fit. The fits always work on the second attempt with the tolerance increased from 0.1 to 1, but looking at the verbose output, I find that the final EDM value is much smaller in the second attempt compared to the first, such that it would have passed the initial tolerance as well. I put a verbose output in [3], where you see this effect (This output corresponds to a 1D scan that's slightly different from the command I posted above). I find the same feature when starting by using the strategy 1, and using the fallback to the strategy 0 with tolerance 1, but in those cases the status 4 of the failed fits is more common than status 3.
Could you please let me know, if these are known features, and in particular, what could be done to fix the issue of my first problem, to get a more accurate result on the initial result of MultiDimFit? I was wondering if it would help, if I could allow Minuit to increase the number of calls, but I don't see an option in combine to set this.
Cheers,
Dennis
[1] /afs/cern.ch/user/d/dmroy/public/Combine_25.10.21/WSRun2_unblind.indep.root
[2] https://hypernews.cern.ch/HyperNews/CMS/get/AUX/2021/10/25/11:05:10-3631-1DScanVBF.png
[3] /afs/cern.ch/user/d/dmroy/public/Combine_25.10.21/VerboseOutput
posts.cooked_html:
*** Discussion title: Higgs Combination

Dear experts,

I'd like ask two questions about some observations I made while using combine:

First, I used my workspace to make a 1D scan over the POI. I used this command (with combine tag v8.2.0):

combine -d WSRun2_unblind.indep.root --algo grid -P sigma --setParameterRanges sigma=0,0.05 --alignEdges 1 --points 51 --setParameters fvbf=1.0 --freezeParameters fvbf --cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 0 --cminFallbackAlgo Minuit2,0:1 -M MultiDimFit -m 650

I put the workspace in [1]. The result I get from the scan is [2]. You can see that there's a bump at the minimum. The peak of this bump is the best fit from the initial fit (with NLL=0) before the POI is scanned over the given POI range. The NLL values from the 1D scan surrounding the best fit value are however negative, implying that they were fitted better. Another thing to point out, is that the uncertainty on the POI from the initial fit (or from MultiDimFit with "--algo none", which I think is equivalent) is lower than what can be read from the plot (in this example the uncertainty on the POI is 0.001752, while in the plot it's closer to 0.003).
It seems to me that the accuracy of the fit in the initial fit to data wasn't optimal. Could this be because I'm using the option "--cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 0"? I use this option because there are very many nuisances in my workspace, so it's much faster, and in the past I also found the fits to be more robust and failing less. Though I find it strange that, even when using this same option to scan the range of the POI, NLL values surrounding the best fit are still smaller.

Secondly, I also made a strange observation with the option "--cminFallbackAlgo Minuit2,0:1". In my workspace, the fits sometimes fail with the status 3 or 4, because the EDM value is still too large after the fit. The fits always work on the second attempt with the tolerance increased from 0.1 to 1, but looking at the verbose output, I find that the final EDM value is much smaller in the second attempt compared to the first, such that it would have passed the initial tolerance as well. I put a verbose output in [3], where you see this effect (This output corresponds to a 1D scan that's slightly different from the command I posted above). I find the same feature when starting by using the strategy 1, and using the fallback to the strategy 0 with tolerance 1, but in those cases the status 4 of the failed fits is more common than status 3.

Could you please let me know, if these are known features, and in particular, what could be done to fix the issue of my first problem, to get a more accurate result on the initial result of MultiDimFit? I was wondering if it would help, if I could allow Minuit to increase the number of calls, but I don't see an option in combine to set this.

Cheers,
Dennis

[1] /afs/cern.ch/user/d/dmroy/public/Combine_25.10.21/WSRun2_unblind.indep.root
[2] https://hypernews.cern.ch/HyperNews/CMS/get/AUX/2021/10/25/11:05:10-3631-1DScanVBF.png
[3] /afs/cern.ch/user/d/dmroy/public/Combine_25.10.21/VerboseOutput

posts.name: Nick Smith
posts.text:
Hi,
You can increase the number of calls with "--X-rtd MINIMIZER_MaxCalls=999999999"
As for the rest of your question, perhaps the initial fit found the bump to be a saddle point and terminated at that point? Did the initial fit have a good (i.e. positive-definite) result from HESSE? (iirc MultiDimFit does not usually run HESSE, but FitDiagnostics will)

--cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy

This parameter does adjust how MIGRAD estimates the inverse hessian matrix at each step in the algorithm (quasi-newton method). So it is certainly possible that it leads to an estimate that is positive-definite at the saddle point but a full hessian computation would have shown the minimum is not valid. If for example you set the strategy to 2, then each step attempts to compute the full hessian inverse, which of course is very expensive.
~Nick
posts.cooked_html:
Hi,

You can increase the number of calls with "--X-rtd MINIMIZER_MaxCalls=999999999"
As for the rest of your question, perhaps the initial fit found the bump to be a saddle point and terminated at that point? Did the initial fit have a good (i.e. positive-definite) result from HESSE? (iirc MultiDimFit does not usually run HESSE, but FitDiagnostics will)

--cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy

This parameter does adjust how MIGRAD estimates the inverse hessian matrix at each step in the algorithm (quasi-newton method). So it is certainly possible that it leads to an estimate that is positive-definite at the saddle point but a full hessian computation would have shown the minimum is not valid. If for example you set the strategy to 2, then each step attempts to compute the full hessian inverse, which of course is very expensive.

~Nick

posts.name: Dennis Mario Roy
posts.text:
*** Discussion title: Higgs Combination
Hi Nick,
Thank you for your response! By increasing the number of calls I indeed get less failing fits now.
Whenever I run FitDiagnostics, I do so with "--robustHesse 1" option. Then I usually get messages "covariance matrix quality: Full matrix, but forced positive-definite" and "Note: the following parameters were dropped to make the covariance matrix pos-def:", where one or two nuisances are dropped.
Looking at the combine code, MultiDimFit also makes use of HESSE for "--algo singles". Running this I got no errors ("covariance matrix quality: Full, accurate covariance matrix"), and this also gave me an uncertainty on the POI that was in line with the uncertainty from the scan. I was wondering: Is the error I get from only the best fit (e.g. obtained from "--trackErrors rgx{.*}") not accurate compared to using the singles algorithm or the 1D scan?
Lastly, I'm also still running some fits with "--cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 2", but so far it looks like it always returns the status code 1 or 3. In the case of status 3, I see the same feature again where, after only increasing the tolerance by a factor 10 due to the fallback, the EDM of the next fit is anyhow lower again such that it would have passed the initial tolerance.
Cheers,
Dennis
posts.cooked_html:
*** Discussion title: Higgs Combination

Hi Nick,

Thank you for your response! By increasing the number of calls I indeed get less failing fits now.

Whenever I run FitDiagnostics, I do so with "--robustHesse 1" option. Then I usually get messages "covariance matrix quality: Full matrix, but forced positive-definite" and "Note: the following parameters were dropped to make the covariance matrix pos-def:", where one or two nuisances are dropped.

Looking at the combine code, MultiDimFit also makes use of HESSE for "--algo singles". Running this I got no errors ("covariance matrix quality: Full, accurate covariance matrix"), and this also gave me an uncertainty on the POI that was in line with the uncertainty from the scan. I was wondering: Is the error I get from only the best fit (e.g. obtained from "--trackErrors rgx{.*}") not accurate compared to using the singles algorithm or the 1D scan?

Lastly, I'm also still running some fits with "--cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 2", but so far it looks like it always returns the status code 1 or 3. In the case of status 3, I see the same feature again where, after only increasing the tolerance by a factor 10 due to the fallback, the EDM of the next fit is anyhow lower again such that it would have passed the initial tolerance.

Cheers,
Dennis
